lines.en_lit: Come hear Rabbi Yehuda says: there was no lottery the coal pan, Rather, the priest who won of the incense said to the one who was next to him: Be privileged along with me with the coal pan.
The coal pan and incense are different, because they are a single service,
There are who say specifically the coal pan and the incense, which are one service, However, other services, require a lottery
it was necessary for him have entered your mind to say that since is infrequent, and it brings about wealth we should institute a lottery for itself, teaches us

Come hear Rabbi Ḥiyya taught A lottery is not held for each individual service; rather, priest who won the lottery of the daily offering, twelve of his fellow priests, are drawn in along with him Learn from this
The second lottery A dilemma was raised before Who collects slaughters collects that if you say sprinkles collects due to his love not collect all the blood
Or perhaps sprinkles collects as, if you say slaughters collects, sometimes a non-priest slaughters
Come hear Ben Katin made twelve spigots for the laver so that his twelve fellow priests who were engaged in the daily offering could sanctify their hands and feet at one time.
And if it should enter your mind slaughters collects there are thirteen Rather, isn’t it conclude from this sprinkles, collects Conclude from this
Rav Aḥa, son of Rava, said to Rav Ashi: We too have learned The slaughterer slaughtered, the collector collected, and he comes to sprinkle Conclude from this

ben Azzai said before Rabbi Akiva The Sages taught In what manner the way it walks? The head and the leg The breast and the neck, and the two forelegs, and the two flanks, the tail and the leg. Rabbi Yosei says: It was sacrificed the way it was skinned. In what manner the way it was skinned? The head and the leg the tail and the leg two flanks, and the two forelegs, the breast and the neck.

Rabbi Akiva says: It was sacrificed the way it was cut up In what manner the way it was cut up? The head and the leg and the two forelegs, the breast and the neck, and the two flanks, and the tail and the leg. Rabbi Yosei HaGelili says: It was sacrificed according to quality In what manner the quality The head and the leg, the breast and the neck, and the two flanks, the tail and the leg, and two forelegs.
But isn’t it written: “Every good piece, the thigh and the shoulder” That lean
Rava said: Both the tanna our and Rabbi Yosei we follow the quality of the meat Sage, follows the limb, of the meat; Sage, follows the fattiness of the meat.
what is the reason that leg goes up together with the head? Because the head has much bone in it, the leg is offered with it.

at any rate everyone agrees head is sacrificed first. From where do we As it was taught From where that the head and the fat precede all the limbs? The verse states: and its head and its fat, and shall arrange And the other fat
